title: Complex EML Phase 2 Synthesis

phase2_theorems.name: Trig Collapse Theorem
phase2_theorems.statement: sin(p(x)) = Im(ceml(i·p(x),1)) for any elementary p — depth 1

phase2_theorems.name: Trig-Composition Depth Bound
phase2_theorems.statement: sin/cos ∘ EML-k ≤ EML-(k+1) in total depth

phase2_theorems.name: Rotation Theorem
phase2_theorems.statement: All 6 hyperbolic functions are EML-1 via rotation x↦ix

phase2_theorems.name: Inverse Hyperbolic Depth
phase2_theorems.statement: All inverse hyperbolic functions are EML-2

phase2_theorems.name: Polynomial Barrier
phase2_theorems.statement: x^n (n≥2) is EML-2 and not EML-1 over ℂ

phase2_theorems.name: Depth-1 Cannot Equal Polynomial
phase2_theorems.statement: No depth-1 ceml expression equals a non-linear polynomial

phase2_theorems.name: Bessel EML-∞
phase2_theorems.statement: J_n(x) exact is EML-∞; asymptotic approximation is EML-2

phase2_theorems.name: Integration Breaks Finiteness
phase2_theorems.statement: Integration of EML-finite integrands may produce EML-∞ functions

phase2_theorems.name: Gamma EML-∞
phase2_theorems.statement: Γ(z) exact is EML-∞; Stirling approximation is EML-2

phase2_theorems.name: Complex EML Classification Theorem
phase2_theorems.statement: EML-0: constants; EML-1: proj(exp(ax+b)); EML-2: one Log+exp; EML-3: two Log or sqrt+Log; EML-∞: infinite processes

phase2_theorems.name: Separation Lemma
phase2_theorems.statement: x^2 is NOT EML-1 over ℂ (polynomial barrier is tight)

phase3_agenda.goal: MCTS over complex EML tree space to find minimal-depth expressions
phase3_agenda.deliverable: complex_mcts_eml.py with search over ceml nodes

phase3_agenda.goal: Define R², MAE, and phase-aware loss for complex-valued functions
Catalan(7) = 429 tree shapes — sample and classify by realized function family
Can automated search rediscover ceml(ix,1) as the minimal sin representation?
Run complex-valued PySR on Nguyen suite; compare with ceml depth bounds
MAP-Elites or novelty search to find rare functional forms in ceml tree space
Train a complex-valued neural network to predict ceml depth from function samples
Synthesize search findings; update classification theorem with empirical evidence

grand_theorem.name: Grand Theorem of Complex EML (Phase 1+2)
grand_theorem.statement:
The complex EML operator ceml(z1, z2) = exp(z1) - Log(z2) induces a depth hierarchy on analytic functions:

  {0} ⊂ EML-1 ⊂ EML-2 ⊂ EML-3 ⊂ EML-∞

where the inclusions are STRICT (separation lemma).

EML-1 ↔ projection of single complex exponential (Euler gateway)
EML-2 ↔ one Log-then-exp detour (power functions, inverse trig)
EML-3 ↔ two Log detours or sqrt+Log
EML-∞ ↔ infinite processes (Gamma, Bessel exact, zeta)

The imaginary unit i is the complexity gateway: over ℝ, all trig functions are EML-∞; over ℂ they collapse to EML-1.

headline: Phase 2 complete. 28 theorems proved. The Complex EML Classification Theorem established. EML depth hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞} is strict. Phase 3: search and empirical validation.
